meta.setting: Spring caves, seep water, mineral stains, mine-adjacent hollows, buckets, and disputed water rights in Jefferson country
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with hidden water, claim pressure, cave danger, and practical route knowledge

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Mining, settlement, road travel, and rugged water pressure around Whiskeytown.

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Respectful regional grounding for Native presence and long knowledge of local waterways.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Spring Caves region page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: H3 local marker line in the register of Seep Stone, Bucket Rope, and Cold Breath
square image reference immediately after the marker line
opening blockquote built as three short generated lines: cave object, water or air detail, plain table meaning
two compact paragraphs establishing spring caves as water source, hiding place, claim pressure, route clue, and physical hazard
closing blockquote that gives a generated spring-cave sign and practical table meaning
generation_guidance: Generate spring caves as practical hidden water, not mystery scenery: seep stone, mineral crust, bucket rope, bad footing, lamp smoke, cold air, and the sound of water behind rock.
Give the table hooks around a poisoned bucket, disputed water access, a hidden cache, a trapped animal, claim men guarding the mouth, or tracks that vanish on wet stone.
Include respectful room for Wintu or other Native families who know seasonal water and old routes, without inventing ceremonies, sacred details, or made-up language.
Make water useful and dangerous; the cave can save a camp, expose a lie, chill a horse, or turn a gunshot deafening.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: The cave should feel valuable because a thirsty man can count the drops.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A spring-cave scene should ask who knows the water, who controls the entrance, what was lowered by rope, and what the wet stone kept from showing.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western water, cave, claim, and trail language only.
The generated page must include image path images/spring-caves.jpg.
The prompt must not contain final prose, final blockquotes, or finished field marks.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not make the cave mystical, sacred-invented, enchanted, or heroic
do not romanticize Native people or invent cultural details
do not include modern pumps, plastic buckets, electric lights, safety railings, or recreational cave-tour language
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about spring caves as hidden water, hazard, claim pressure, and scene engine.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
